# Azure Key Vault Connector - Full Documentation

# Azure Key Vault Connector 1.2 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show several Mule flows for Azure Key Vault Connector:

- [Encrypt and Decrypt a Secret](#encrypt-and-decrypt-a-secret)
- [Retrieve and Save a Certificate](#retrieve-and-save-a-certificate)
- [Retrieve a Secret and Its Metadata](#retrieve-a-secret-and-its-metadata)

## Configure a Connection

To secure connections, you must specify the connection field values. To do this:

- [Create a configuration file for a connection.](#create-config-file)
- [Configure the connection global elements.](#configure-conn)
- [Configure a global element for the properties file.](#configure-global)

### Create a Configuration File for a Connection

Create a configuration file that includes properties for a connection:

1.  Create a file named `mule-app.properties` in the `/src/main/resources/` folder.
2.  In the `mule-app.properties` file, create a set of properties for the connection, similar to the
    ones that follow, replacing the bracketed text (including the brackets) with the correct values
    for your configuration:

    azure.keyVault.tenantId=<tenant ID> azure.keyVault.clientId=<client ID>
    azure.keyVault.clientSecret=<client secret> azure.keyVault.vaultName=<vault name>

    azure.keyVault.secret=<secret> azure.keyVault.key=<key> azure.keyVault.certificate=<certificate>

    your-directory=<your directory>

For more information about creating a properties file, refer to
[Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

### Configure the Connection Global Elements

Configure global elements for connection:

1.  Create a new Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange** and enter `Azure Key Vault`.
3.  Add **Azure Key Vault Connector** to the **Selected modules** section and click **Finish**.
4.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
5.  Select **Connector Configuration > Azure Key Vault Config** and click **OK**.
6.  Enter the values to configure the Connection authentication type.
7.  Click the **Test Connection** button to ensure there is connectivity with the Azure Key Vault
    API. A successful message should pop up.
8.  Click **OK**.

### Configure a Global Element for the Properties File

Configure a global element for the `mule-app.properties` file so that Mule knows where to find it:

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
2.  In the **Choose Global Type** dialog, select **Configuration properties** and click **OK**.
3.  In the **File** field, enter `mule.app.properties`.
4.  Click **OK**.

## Encrypt and Decrypt a Secret

This Mule flow retrieves a secret, encrypts and then decrypts the secret using the key provided in
the configuration file, and logs the whole process.

This example uses the following components:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Get Secret**  
  Retrieves a secret
- **Logger**  
  Logs the secret value
- **Encrypt Message**  
  Encrypts a message
- **Logger**  
  Encrypts the log in base64
- **Decrypt Message**  
  Decrypts a message
- **Transform Message**  
  Transforms the message using the algorithm
- **Logger**  
  Decrypts the log

![Studio flow for the Encrypt and Decrypt a Secret flow](_images/encrypt-decrypt.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:azure-key-vault="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault/current/mule-azure-key-vault.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
	<azure-key-vault:config name="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" doc:name="Azure Key Vault Config" >
		<azure-key-vault:connection clientId="${azure.keyVault.clientId}" tenantId="${azure.keyVault.tenantId}" clientSecret="${azure.keyVault.clientSecret}" vaultName="${azure.keyVault.vaultName}" >
			<azure-key-vault:crypto-client-config />
		</azure-key-vault:connection>
	</azure-key-vault:config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="daa0beda-3e51-4965-9adf-3a328d38623e" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config" doc:id="e2650ca2-1343-41ae-a330-f67b3569362d" >
		<file:connection workingDir="${your-directory}" />
	</file:config>
	<flow name="EncryptDecryptFlow" doc:id="90e8e344-f5b5-4dae-a4e2-2e419e02b8c0" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="f6ad0772-95b6-44e6-827e-0ea009b2eac6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/encryptDecrypt" />
		<azure-key-vault:get-secret doc:name="Get Secret" doc:id="b8e3d495-266e-4944-9af3-c485023c732b" config-ref="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" name="${azure.keyVault.secret}"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log secret value" doc:id="2693df8f-57a9-49fb-b1f9-58767037d206" message="#[%dw 2.0&#10;output application/json&#10;---&#10;payload.value]" />
		<azure-key-vault:encrypt-message algorithm="RSA_OAEP" doc:name="Encrypt Message" doc:id="e5d88934-b4d2-45b3-9df6-df529d43ab2c" config-ref="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" keyName="${azure.keyVault.key}" >
			<azure-key-vault:message ><![CDATA[#[payload.value]]]></azure-key-vault:message>
		</azure-key-vault:encrypt-message>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log encrypetd in base 64" doc:id="d628751a-9125-429d-b3c2-0ab3d2cbdf1a" message="#[%dw 2.0&#10;import * from dw::core::Binaries&#10;output text/plain&#10;---&#10;toBase64(payload.data)]" />
		<azure-key-vault:decrypt-message algorithm="RSA_OAEP" doc:name="Decrypt Message" doc:id="96a8e168-4989-4b56-be15-5e5494ce8292" config-ref="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" keyName="${azure.keyVault.key}" >
			<azure-key-vault:message ><![CDATA[#[payload.data]]]></azure-key-vault:message>
		</azure-key-vault:decrypt-message>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="0848025d-900b-4e7f-a236-ff06089ca82c" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	algorithm: payload.encryptionAlgorithm,
	value: payload.data
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log decrypted" doc:id="020e8473-f80f-4356-8b06-8304a8650eaf" message="#[payload]" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Retrieve and Save a Certificate

This Mule flow retrieves a certificate and saves the certificate into a file.

This example uses the following components:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Get Certificate**  
  Retrieves a certificate
- **Write**  
  Writes the content into the file
- **Transform Message**  
  Outputs the success message that says the certificate is saved into a file

![Studio flow for the Retrieve and Save a Certificate flow](_images/retrieve-save.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:azure-key-vault="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault/current/mule-azure-key-vault.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
	<azure-key-vault:config name="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" doc:name="Azure Key Vault Config" >
		<azure-key-vault:connection clientId="${azure.keyVault.clientId}" tenantId="${azure.keyVault.tenantId}" clientSecret="${azure.keyVault.clientSecret}" vaultName="${azure.keyVault.vaultName}" >
			<azure-key-vault:crypto-client-config />
		</azure-key-vault:connection>
	</azure-key-vault:config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="daa0beda-3e51-4965-9adf-3a328d38623e" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config" doc:id="e2650ca2-1343-41ae-a330-f67b3569362d" >
		<file:connection workingDir="${your-directory}" />
	</file:config>
  <flow name="GetCertificateAndSaveIntoFileFlow" doc:id="fc506299-1d81-4a24-ba9f-c05945406c78" >
  <http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="460dab77-913a-46a0-9b71-b1efc6053699" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/getCertificate" />
  <azure-key-vault:get-certificate doc:name="Get Certificate" doc:id="f2d1eacf-f017-413b-9dfb-0871b975ade8" config-ref="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" name="${azure.keyVault.certificate}" />
  <file:write doc:id="c2b81872-03c4-4590-bda4-a6b8cd585347" config-ref="File_Config" path="auth_certificate.cer" doc:name="Write" >
    <file:content ><![CDATA[#[payload.certificate]]]></file:content>
  </file:write>
  <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="ff4ecd1d-7154-4d08-9adc-b66f77e78f29" >
    <ee:message >
      <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
message: "file created"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
    </ee:message>
  </ee:transform>
</flow>
</mule>
```

## Retrieve a Secret and Its Metadata

This Mule flow retrieves a secret and its metadata.

This example uses the following components:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Get secret metadata**  
  Retrieves the metadata of the secret
- **Transform Message**  
  Transforms metadata to JSON
- **Logger**  
  Logs the message

![Studio flow for the Retrieve a Secret and Its Metadata flow](_images/retrieve-secret.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:azure-key-vault="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault/current/mule-azure-key-vault.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
	<azure-key-vault:config name="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" doc:name="Azure Key Vault Config" >
		<azure-key-vault:connection clientId="${azure.keyVault.clientId}" tenantId="${azure.keyVault.tenantId}" clientSecret="${azure.keyVault.clientSecret}" vaultName="${azure.keyVault.vaultName}" >
			<azure-key-vault:crypto-client-config />
		</azure-key-vault:connection>
	</azure-key-vault:config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="daa0beda-3e51-4965-9adf-3a328d38623e" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config" doc:id="e2650ca2-1343-41ae-a330-f67b3569362d" >
		<file:connection workingDir="${your-directory}" />
	</file:config>
  <flow name="GetSecretMetadataFlow" doc:id="809ade24-8404-4c73-bd7a-6ec3b74f7423" >
  <http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="403a1ae3-cdb3-4ce7-8ff7-a8cba2ad21e6" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/getSecretMetadata" />
  <azure-key-vault:get-secret doc:name="Get secret metadata" doc:id="003da980-2b2e-428a-86b4-b8598937cbb3" config-ref="Azure_Key_Vault_Config" name="${azure.keyVault.secret}" />
  <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Metadata to JSON" doc:id="24b0285e-ee74-43dc-a46b-376d3b8f19b8" >
    <ee:message >
      <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
    </ee:message>
  </ee:transform>
  <logger level="INFO" doc:name="" doc:id="91bf36b7-f4c4-4cdf-b827-55a2bec8a3ce" message="#[payload]" />
</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Key Vault Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Azure Key Vault (Azure Key Vault Connector) enables you to retrieve secrets,
keys, or certificates, and encrypt or decrypt content.

## Configuration

Default Configuration for Azure Key Vault Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Connection">Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection - Connection Type

Uses a client ID, client secret, and cryptography client cache for authentication. Note that the
connector configuration does not allow properties such as `client_id` and `client_secret` to be hard
coded.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Client Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Tenant ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client secret.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Vault Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Vault name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Uri Override</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URI override for the key vault. If you configure this field, then the value for the Vault Name field is not considered for the connection configuration. If the key vault is not located on Azure Commercial Cloud, then configure this field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Azure Default Credential</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Uses the authentication parameters taken from environment variables.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cryptography Client Cache</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#CachedCryptographyClientConfiguration">Cached Cryptography Client Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The cryptography client is created based on a key. If the key does not change (no new versions are created), the client is cached and the <strong>Encrypt Message</strong> and <strong>Decrypt Message</strong> operations take less time. When encrypting or decrypting a message, a key must be retrieved internally. Retrieving a key every time makes the operation take longer to execute. Enable <strong>Cryptography Client Cache</strong> to save time when retrieving the key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Decrypt Message](#DecryptMessage)
- [Encrypt Message](#EncryptMessage)
- [Get Certificate](#GetCertificate)
- [Get Secret](#GetSecret)

### Decrypt Message

`<azure-key-vault:decrypt-message>`

Decrypts a message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RSA_OAEP</p></li><li><p>RSA_OAEP_256</p></li><li><p>RSA1_5</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Algorithm type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key version.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message to decrypt.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CryptographyResult">Cryptography Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:DECRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:ENCRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_VAULT
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Encrypt Message

`<azure-key-vault:encrypt-message>`

Encrypts a message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RSA_OAEP</p></li><li><p>RSA_OAEP_256</p></li><li><p>RSA1_5</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Algorithm type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key version.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message to encrypt.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#CryptographyResult">Cryptography Result</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:DECRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:ENCRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_VAULT
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Get Certificate

`<azure-key-vault:get-certificate>`

Retrieves a certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate version.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#VaultCertificate">Vault Certificate</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:DECRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:ENCRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_VAULT
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Get Secret

`<azure-key-vault:get-secret>`

Retrieves a secret.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret version.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#VaultSecret">Vault Secret</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:DECRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:ENCRYPT_ERROR
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:INVALID_VAULT
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-KEY-VAULT:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Object Types

- [Cached Cryptography Client Configuration](#CachedCryptographyClientConfiguration)
- [Cryptography Result](#CryptographyResult)
- [Expiration Policy](#ExpirationPolicy)
- [Pooling Profile](#PoolingProfile)
- [Reconnect](#Reconnect)
- [Reconnect Forever](#ReconnectForever)
- [Reconnection](#Reconnection)
- [Vault Certificate](#VaultCertificate)
- [Vault Secret](#VaultSecret)

### Cached Cryptography Client Configuration

Configures the cached cryptography client configuration.

The cryptography client is created based on a key. If the key does not change (no new versions are
created), the client is cached and the **Encrypt Message** and **Decrypt Message** operations take
less time.

When encrypting or decrypting a message, a key must be retrieved internally. Retrieving a key every
time makes the operation take longer to execute. Enable **Cryptography Client Cache** to save time
when retrieving the key.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Cached Crypto Client Ttl</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Cached crypto client time-to-live (TTL) value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Cached Crypto Client Ttl</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MINUTES</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Cryptography Result

Configures the cryptography result type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Data</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Data.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RSA_OAEP</p></li><li><p>RSA_OAEP_256</p></li><li><p>RSA1_5</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Pooling Profile

Configures the pooling profile type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Active</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of Mule components that can be borrowed from a session at one time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of components that are active at one time. When <strong>Max Active</strong> is exceeded, the pool is exhausted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of Mule components that sit idle in the pool at any time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of Mule components that are idle at one time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds to wait for a pooled component to become available when the pool is exhausted and when <strong>Exhausted Action</strong> is set to <code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Eviction Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines the minimum amount of time an object sits idle in the pool before the object is eligible for eviction. When non-positive, no objects are evicted from the pool due to idle time alone.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Eviction Check Interval Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds between runs of the object evictor. When non-positive, no object evictor is executed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Exhausted Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the behavior of the Mule component pool when the pool is exhausted:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL</code></p><div><p>Throws a <code>NoSuchElementException</code></p></div></li><li><p><code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</code></p><div><p>Blocks by invoking Object.wait(long) until a new or idle object is available</p></div></li><li><p><code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</code></p><div><p>Creates a new Mule instance and returns it, essentially making <strong>Max Active</strong> meaningless. If <strong>Max Wait</strong> is positive, <code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</code> blocks for at most that many milliseconds, after which a <code>NoSuchElementException</code> is thrown. If <strong>Max Wait</strong> is negative, <code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</code> blocks indefinitely.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Initialisation Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INITIALISE_NONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how components in a pool should be initialized:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>INITIALISE_NONE</code></p><div><p>Does not load any components into the pool on startup</p></div></li><li><p><code>INITIALISE_ONE</code></p><div><p>Loads one initial component into the pool on startup</p></div></li><li><p><code>INITIALISE_ALL</code></p><div><p>Loads all components in the pool on startup</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether pooling is disabled or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Vault Certificate

Configures the vault certificate type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate content type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Key Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate key type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#VaultSecret">Vault Secret</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate secret.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Created On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Date the vault certificate is created.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the vault certificate is enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expires On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the expiration of the vault certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies which DateTime the vault certificate must be not before.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recovery Level</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Recovery level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Updated On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies which DateTime the vault certificate is updated on.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version of the vault certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Vault Secret

Configures the vault secret type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Created On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Date the vault secret is created.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the vault secret is enabled.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expires On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the expiration of the vault secret.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies which DateTime the vault secret must be not before.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recovery Level</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Recovery level.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Updated On</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies which DateTime the vault secret is updated on.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version of the vault secret.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Azure Key Vault Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, refer to
the [Azure Key Vault Connector Reference](azure-key-vault-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Anypoint Connector for Azure Key Vault (Azure Key Vault Connector) to your Mule project to
automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the
required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `Azure Key Vault` in the search field.
3.  Click **Azure Key Vault Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Azure Key Vault:

- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- Scheduler  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Azure Key Vault Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Azure Key Vault** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Azure Key Vault system. Azure Key Vault Connector
supports the Connection authentication.

To configure the global element for Azure Key Vault Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the [Connection](#connection) authentication
    method to configure.

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, refer to
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### Connection Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Connection authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Client Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the client ID.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the tenant ID.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the client secret.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Vault Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the vault name.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Azure Key Vault Connector, configure the other required
fields for the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Azure Key Vault Reference](azure-key-vault-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Key Vault Connector 1.2 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault/current/mule-azure-key-vault.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
    xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:azure-key-vault="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-key-vault/current/mule-azure-key-vault.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule4-azure-key-vault-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `Azure Key Vault`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Key Vault Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Azure Key Vault (Azure Key Vault Connector) enables you to retrieve secrets,
keys, or certificates, and encrypt or decrypt content.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the Azure Key Vault Connector Release
Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Azure Key Vault Secrets API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Azure Key Vault target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Some common use cases for Azure Key Vault Connector include:

- Authenticating to Azure Key Vault via Client Secret Credential to access Key Vault secrets without
  exposing sensitive credentials in code, thus improving resilience and security
- Enabling other applications in a Mule flow to have access to encrypt and decrypt operations using
  Azure Key Vault keys to avoid key rotations
- Authenticating to other database and HTTP connectors in a more secure and automated way to fetch
  and use credentials to access other endpoints
- Retrieving a certificate and saving the certificate into a file

## Connection Types

Azure Key Vault connections use the Connection authentication type, which uses a client ID, client
secret, and cryptography client cache.

## Environment Variables

When setting **Use Azure Default Credential** to `True`, you must set one of the following
environment variables `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET`, or `AZURE_TENANT_ID`. Refer to
[Setting environment variables](../../mule-runtime/latest/setting-environment-variables)
documentation for details.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Azure Key Vault Connector Reference](azure-key-vault-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
